summary:
Measuring serum FSH helps distinguish between testicular failure and obstructive conditions by indicating intrinsically impaired sperm production or not. An elevated FSH suggests a problem within the testes themselves instead of an issue along the reproductive tract pathway leading from them. To summarize further:

Measuring serum FSH can help identify whether low sperm count results from problems inside the testicles versus obstructions elsewhere; high levels indicate internal damage affecting sperm creation directly. Sentence two focuses more broadly while still capturing essential information about using FSH measurement as part of differential diagnosis based primarily upon where fertility impairment originates—within vs outside the testis itself. Agent1 concluded that testing serum FSH would be useful because higher-than-normal values suggest underlying issues specific to the testes' ability to produce sperm cells effectively when there isn't An elevated FSH suggests a problem within the testes themselves instead of an issue along the reproductive tract pathway leading from them. To summarize further:

Measuring serum FSH can help identify whether low sperm count results from problems inside the testicles versus obstructions elsewhere; high levels indicate internal damage affecting sperm creation directly. Sentence two focuses more broadly while still capturing essential information about using FSH measurement as part of differential diagnosis based primarily upon where fertility impairment originates—within vs outside the testis itself.
